channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian Labor Party adopts interventionist AI strategy
item.description: The Albanese government is shifting towards a more interventionist approach in artificial intelligence, aiming to address public concerns about the technology's impact. This strategy involves compelli...

item.description: According to a detailed report by the Organized Crime and Corruption Reporting Project (OCCRP), an Indian-origin businessman named Gaurav Srivastava has been accused of impersonating a Central Intelli...

item.description: The article outlines Kenya's new system for assigning students to senior secondary schools based on their performance in the Kenya Junior Secondary Education Assessment (KJSEA) and School-Based Assess...

item.title: Blood vs Duty: Lara's Emotional Revelation in Episode 66

item.title: All On's 10th Anniversary Event Highlights Renewable Energy Collaborations and Future Plans in Nigeria

item.title: Four Injured in East L.A. Shooting Following Mexico-England World Cup Match
item.description: Four people were wounded by gunfire in East Los Angeles after crowds gathered following the Mexico-England World Cup soccer game on Sunday night. According to the Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Departme...

item.title: Tragedy of Three-Year-Old Boy Found Dead After Being Swept Away by Stream Near Taliparamba
item.description: A three-year-old boy, Najuwan Ahmad, was tragically swept away by a strong current in a stream near Poomangalam, close to Taliparamba, Kerala. The incident occurred on Sunday evening when the child ac...

item.title: NATO Summit in Turkey Focuses on Military Spending and Diplomatic Tensions
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney and U.S. President Donald Trump are set to attend the annual NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, where leaders will address rising military budgets and diplomatic challenges. The...

item.title: Community Efforts to Commemorate Whistle-Blower Babita Deokaran with Monument
item.description: The 'Honouring a Hero – Babita Deokaran' BackaBuddy campaign is raising funds to unveil a permanent monument for Babita Deokaran, a former Chief Director of Financial Accounting at the Gauteng Departm...

item.title: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit makes public appearance after lung transplant, marks World Cup victory
item.description: The article highlights the recent public appearance of Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit following a life-saving lung transplant. She was seen at Wembley for the first time since her surgery, coinci...

item.title: Sen. Ted Cruz Acknowledges Trump's Involvement in FIFA World Cup Decisions During Oval Office Event
item.description: During a ceremony marking the launch of 'Trump Accounts' in the White House, Sen. Ted Cruz (R-TX) openly admitted to President Donald Trump's alleged meddling in a FIFA World Cup game decision, sparki...

item.title: Richmound village moves forward after cult departure with greenhouse project
item.description: The village of Richmound, Saskatchewan, held a street party to mark a new era after two years under the influence of the Kingdom of Canada cult. Mayor Brad Miller symbolically broke ground on a commun...

item.title: Gilas Pilipinas Suffers Heavy Defeat Against Australia in Basketball Match
item.description: In a devastating performance during the FIBA World Cup Asian Qualifiers, Gilas Pilipinas suffered a crushing 43-point loss to Australia. The match highlighted significant defensive lapses and poor bal...

item.title: Christian Eriksen faces medical scrutiny after second pitch collapse as Ukraine friendly is abandoned
item.description: Professional footballer Christian Eriksen experienced a second cardiac episode during a match for Denmark, leading to the abandonment of a friendly against Ukraine. This incident occurs nearly five ye...
